title: Crunchy, Char-grilled Japanese Tofu Miso Oden
description: A briny, crumbly, and savory Kyushu-style Japanese dish featuring crispy tofu, pine nuts, and a rich lacquer glaze.
instructions: Cut the tofu into small cubes and season with salt.
In a small saucepan, whisk together miso paste, soy sauce, sake, rice vinegar, sugar, and grated ginger.
Bring the sauce to a simmer over medium heat and cook until thickened, about 5 minutes.
Heat a comal or griddle over medium-high heat.
Add the tofu to the comal and cook until crispy and golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
In a small bowl, mix together pine nuts and a pinch of salt.
Sprinkle the pine nuts over the tofu and cook for an additional minute.
Brush the lacquer glaze over the tofu and cook for an additional 30 seconds.
Transfer the tofu to a serving platter and drizzle with sesame oil.
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
